findings.justification: The response includes 'fullPath' => $path, exposing the absolute server filesystem path to any authenticated admin user.

When realpath() resolves outside $root, the code allows $originalFullPath directly if a symlink is detected in any parent directory, enabling an attacker who can create or predict a symlink to list files outside the intended images directory.
The same symlink bypass logic in actionUpload allows uploading files to a directory outside $root when a symlink ancestor is detected, bypassing the intended path containment.

User-controlled $info['extension'] (derived from the uploaded filename) and $mediaMime (from mime_content_type on a user-supplied file) are interpolated unescaped into an error notification string, which may be rendered as HTML in the admin UI.
SVG files are explicitly allowed as an upload type; SVG can contain embedded JavaScript that executes in the browser context of any user who views the uploaded file, constituting stored XSS.

Calls to $this->response->error() inside displayError() and early checks do not appear to halt PHP execution (no return/exit after the call), so code may continue to execute past error conditions including partial/failed uploads.
